item.title: Two shipments of Russian oil and gas head to Cuba in defiance of US
item.description: First cargo on course to arrive on Monday as Donald Trump taunts communist island while administration engages in talks

item.title: Game bird shooting in England faces stricter regulation
item.description: Government seeks to tighten rules governing the release of pheasants, grouse and partridges into the wild

item.title: Ingredients for a successful LBO: lots of equity, and some Trump stardust
item.description: The financing of Electronic Arts is the biggest of its kind since the financial crisis

item.title: Ithaca says policy clarity puts Cambo oil and gas project closer to approval
item.description: Company suggests certainty over future tax regime is helping unlock one of UK North Sea’s biggest developments

item.title: Iran vows to retaliate against Gulf energy after largest gasfield hit
item.description: South Pars provides most of Iran’s domestic gas and is critical to its electricity generation

item.title: Green Party leader pledges £8.4bn to stop UK energy bills rising
item.description: Zack Polanski says party would prevent increases in household energy costs through higher taxes on capital gains

item.title: Economists raise UK inflation forecasts after energy costs surge
item.description: Bank of England now expected to keep interest rates on hold at its meeting on Thursday

item.title: MFS owner Paresh Raja hit with worldwide freezing order
item.description: Businessman barred from spending more than £5,000 a week without administrators’ consent

item.title: No FT, no alpha
item.description: Your most reliable source for US biotech M&A, apparently

item.title: How can the UK shield households from higher energy bills?
item.description: Keir Starmer has made clear the government is unlikely to intervene at scale until higher prices hit consumers

item.title: Kirkland defies private equity downturn with record $11mn partner pay
item.description: Chicago-founded law firm becomes first to break $10bn barrier for annual revenues

item.title: Can AI help me find a home?
item.description: New technology is transforming house-hunting — but will Machine vision and Gaussian splats replace portals and estate agents?

item.title: Trump’s war will boost the clean energy sector he despises
item.description: Turmoil in oil and gas markets is pushing governments to seek alternatives

item.title: Should AI companies have the power to decide if chatbots live or die?
item.description: The final episode of our podcast series examines whether can AI help us cheat death, and if chatbots have a lifespan too

item.title: Cyborg cockroaches are coming to a pipeline near you
item.description: The remote-controlled insects are equipped with cameras to search disaster zones and check for infrastructure damage

item.title: Four challenges for BHP’s incoming chief
item.description: World’s biggest miner taps ‘safe pair of hands’ to navigate copper shift, M&A speculation and tough China environment

item.title: ‘Steroid Olympics’ company to start selling peptides after Kennedy signals deregulation
item.description: Enhanced Games, backed by Donald Trump Jr and Peter Thiel, is moving into consumer products

item.title: UBS completes transfer of 1.2mn Credit Suisse clients in mammoth integration
item.description: Milestone a relief for executives as they battle plans to increase bank’s capital requirements

item.title: Who cares if private credit goes kaput?
item.description: Sizing up the systemic exposure

item.title: FirstFT: Iran war turns shipping market into ‘wild west’
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: UAE tax rules and EU bank capital requirements

item.title: Tech Exchange
item.description: This series features conversations between FT journalists and the technology industry’s leaders, innovators, and thinkers. In the latest instalment, GV’s Tom Hulme talks about the AI investment cycle and predicts a comeback for Google Glass-style smart glasses

item.title: David Zaslav, once upon a time in Hollywood
item.description: The Warner Bros boss could get a $700mn payday from the sale of his studio to Paramount

item.title: Trump has broken it. Now he owns it
item.description: Continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens the movement of gas and fertilisers as well as oil

item.title: Iran still capable of escalating attacks on Gulf’s energy
item.description: Analysts warn Tehran’s ability to prolong disruption to oil and gas flows ‘still has a way to go’

item.title: Iran conflict turns shipping market into ‘wild west’
item.description: Containers are being dropped off at far-flung ports while freight rates have soared

item.title: Banks prepare to offload $18bn in debt tied to EA take-private deal
item.description: Blockbuster junk bond and loan offering will test investor appetite at a time of heightened nerves over AI disruptions

item.title: Now it’s Paramount that faces one battle after another
item.description: Media mogul David Ellison has bagged the prize — but the deal needs to work for shareholders

item.title: Microsoft weighs legal action over $50bn Amazon-OpenAI cloud deal
item.description: Rift deepens as start-up tests limits of Microsoft’s exclusive rights to host its models

item.title: UK’s nuclear research body consults on plans to cut about 200 jobs
item.description: Move by UKNNL leads union to question government claims about ‘golden age’ for sector

item.title: Europe’s airlines eye opportunities from Iran war fallout
item.description: With services of dominant Gulf rivals disrupted, there is a brief window to provide direct flights that avoid the region’s hubs
